best_for: Content-first products that value calm focus
avoid_for: Marketing pages needing high energy or persuasion
effects: Generous spacing, sparse type scale, hairline dividers
a11y_notes: Sparse color can hide interactive affordances so keep visible focus and underlines for links
perf_notes: Very cheap to paint with few assets and small DOM

best_for: Information-dense layouts that need rigorous order
effects: Strict modular grid, flush-left ragged type, strong baseline rhythm
a11y_notes: Tight tracking and small caption sizes can drop below legible thresholds
perf_notes: Lightweight as it leans on layout and type rather than imagery

best_for: Hero panels and overlays over vivid backgrounds
avoid_for: Text-heavy admin screens where clarity matters
effects: Backdrop blur 12-24px, semi-transparent fills, thin light borders
a11y_notes: Translucent text over busy photos often fails contrast so add a solid scrim
perf_notes: Backdrop-filter blur is GPU heavy and can stutter when stacked or scrolled

best_for: Calm control panels and toggle-rich settings
avoid_for: Anything requiring obvious clickability or strong contrast
effects: Dual inner and outer shadows, near-flat surfaces, low color variance
a11y_notes: Low-contrast extruded edges make buttons hard to find and fail WCAG easily
perf_notes: Multiple layered box-shadows raise paint cost on large grids

best_for: Bold portfolios and statement microsites
avoid_for: Trust-sensitive flows like checkout or banking
effects: Visible borders, default link blue, abrupt spacing, no easing
a11y_notes: Default styling can still trip contrast on colored backgrounds so verify pairs
perf_notes: Extremely light since it avoids custom assets and effects

best_for: Playful product launches and creator brands
effects: Solid offset drop shadows, 2-4px black borders, saturated blocks
a11y_notes: Loud color clashes can strain readers so reserve clashing pairs for non-text
perf_notes: Cheap to render but heavy borders and shadows multiply on dense grids

best_for: Onboarding and consumer apps with a soft mood
effects: Big radii, soft double shadows, inflated pastel surfaces
a11y_notes: Pastel-on-pastel can be low contrast so darken text and icons
perf_notes: Soft layered shadows and blurs add moderate paint overhead

best_for: Cross-platform UIs needing speed and consistency
avoid_for: Layouts that need clear depth cues for affordance
effects: No gradients or shadows, bold flat icons, crisp edges
a11y_notes: Flatness removes depth cues so signal interactivity with color states and labels
perf_notes: Among the cheapest to paint with tiny asset footprint

best_for: Productivity apps wanting familiar interaction patterns
avoid_for: Brands wanting a distinctive non-generic identity
effects: Elevation shadows, ripple feedback, 150-300ms motion curves
a11y_notes: Elevation alone is a weak signal so pair with contrast and focus rings
perf_notes: Ripples and shadow layers cost more than flat but stay manageable

best_for: Night-use tools and media or monitoring dashboards
effects: True black backgrounds, restrained accent glows, dim elevation
a11y_notes: Pure white on pure black can cause halation so prefer off-white text
perf_notes: Saves power on OLED but glows and blooms can add compositing work

best_for: Feature showcases and product overview pages
effects: Rounded card tiles, varied spans, gentle hover lift
a11y_notes: Reading order can confuse screen readers so set a logical DOM sequence
perf_notes: Many cards with images need lazy loading to keep first paint fast

best_for: Long-form stories and brand journalism
effects: Multi-column flow, large display serifs, generous leading
a11y_notes: Thin serif weights at small sizes reduce legibility for low vision users
perf_notes: Web fonts and full-bleed imagery dominate weight so subset and compress

best_for: Energetic hero sections for tech and creative brands
effects: Multi-stop mesh gradients, slow drift animation, soft bloom
a11y_notes: Vivid moving gradients hurt text contrast so place copy on solid panels
perf_notes: Animated mesh gradients and filters are GPU-intensive on low-end devices

best_for: Nostalgic tech launches and entertainment sites
effects: Chrome gradients, neon outlines, perspective grids, scanlines
a11y_notes: Neon-on-dark glow can smear small text so keep body copy plain
perf_notes: Glow and grid overlays add fill-rate cost on large viewports

best_for: Fashion and music microsites targeting nostalgia
effects: Glossy bevels, bubble shapes, metallic gradients, sparkle accents
a11y_notes: Busy textures and low-contrast chrome reduce readability for many users
perf_notes: Decorative textures and many small assets inflate page weight

best_for: Gaming UIs and immersive data-viz demos
avoid_for: Calm consumer apps and long reading
effects: Glitch transitions, scanlines, neon edges, monospace readouts
a11y_notes: Neon clutter and glitch motion can trigger discomfort so offer reduced-motion
perf_notes: Glitch shaders and overlays are expensive and can drop frames

best_for: Art and music projects with ironic nostalgia
effects: Pink-cyan gradients, perspective grids, VHS noise, slow pans
a11y_notes: Saturated pastel pairs often fail contrast so isolate text on solid blocks
perf_notes: Noise textures and large gradient images add notable weight

best_for: Youthful brands and event or campaign pages
effects: Scattered geometric shapes, clashing colors, playful patterns
a11y_notes: Busy patterns behind text wreck contrast so keep copy on clean areas
perf_notes: Many vector shapes are light but dense SVG can grow the DOM

best_for: Apps mimicking physical tools for familiarity
effects: Realistic textures, drop shadows, beveled controls, gloss
a11y_notes: Decorative realism can obscure state changes so keep clear focus styles
perf_notes: Detailed textures and bitmaps are the heaviest visual approach

best_for: Storytelling intros and brand statement pages
effects: Staggered text reveals, scroll-triggered timing, easing 200-500ms
a11y_notes: Moving text harms readers with vestibular issues so honor reduced-motion
perf_notes: Continuous text animation forces frequent layout and repaint

best_for: Scrollytelling marketing and product tours
effects: Layered scroll speeds, pinned sections, depth transitions
a11y_notes: Scroll hijacking disorients keyboard and screen-reader users
perf_notes: Multiple scroll-bound layers cause heavy scroll-time repaint

best_for: Wellness sustainability and lifestyle brands
avoid_for: Technical dense or high-density data UIs
effects: Flowing curves, earthy palettes, soft organic blobs, gentle motion
a11y_notes: Low-contrast earthy tones can fail text so darken foreground colors
perf_notes: Organic SVG shapes are light but layered imagery can add weight

best_for: Bold creative brands wanting visual saturation
effects: Overlapping layers, rich patterns, mixed type, busy motion
a11y_notes: Dense visuals overwhelm focus order and contrast so isolate key text
perf_notes: Many layered assets and effects make this paint-heavy and large

best_for: Sophisticated brand sites and portfolios
effects: Single-hue tonal scale, contrast via value, subtle texture
a11y_notes: Color cannot convey state alone so add icons and labels for meaning
perf_notes: Lean palette with minimal assets keeps rendering cheap

best_for: Public services and accessibility-first products
effects: Strong contrast pairs, thick focus rings, clear states
a11y_notes: Designed to exceed WCAG so audit every pair against AAA where possible
perf_notes: Simple high-contrast styling is inexpensive to render

best_for: Premium device-style hero UIs and overlays
avoid_for: Dense text panels and low-power devices
effects: Refractive blur, specular highlights, fluid morph, depth layers
a11y_notes: Refraction over content lowers text contrast so add solid backing
perf_notes: Refraction and dynamic blur are very GPU costly on weak hardware

best_for: Calm consumer dashboards and settings
effects: Subtle shadows, soft radii, muted palette, gentle hovers
a11y_notes: Low contrast and faint edges fail many users so verify minimums
perf_notes: Light shadow use keeps paint cost moderate

best_for: Product launches emphasizing polish and delight
avoid_for: Static reference content and slow networks
effects: Orchestrated entrances, spring physics, 200-400ms transitions
a11y_notes: Heavy motion needs a reduced-motion path and pause controls
perf_notes: Continuous animation taxes the compositor and battery

best_for: Interactive products rewarding small actions
effects: State-driven micro-animations, hover and tap feedback, 100-200ms
a11y_notes: Feedback must not rely on motion alone so pair with text or color
perf_notes: Small targeted animations are cheap if scoped to transform and opacity

best_for: Games indie brands and nostalgic campaigns
effects: Crisp pixel scaling, limited palette, sprite motion, dithering
a11y_notes: Tiny pixel type is hard to read so provide larger UI font for body
perf_notes: Sprites are tiny but nearest-neighbor scaling needs care to stay crisp

best_for: Reading apps and distraction-free writing tools
effects: Paper texture, grayscale, no glow, minimal animation
a11y_notes: Grayscale removes color cues so encode state with shape and text
perf_notes: Extremely light with no heavy effects or large color assets

best_for: Immersive data and product visualization
effects: Z-axis layering, subtle perspective, depth shadows, parallax
a11y_notes: Depth cues can confuse linear navigation so keep DOM order logical
perf_notes: Perspective transforms and layered shadows raise compositing cost

best_for: Underground art and provocative creative work
effects: Intentional misalignment, default elements, jarring contrast
a11y_notes: Deliberate chaos often breaks contrast and focus order so test carefully
perf_notes: Minimal assets make it light despite the raw look

best_for: B2B SaaS and enterprise marketing
effects: Neutral palette, structured grid, subtle shadows, restrained motion
a11y_notes: Neutral grays can drift below contrast minimums so check body text
perf_notes: Conservative styling keeps assets and paint cost low

best_for: Premium fashion jewelry and hospitality brands
avoid_for: Budget mass-market or dense utility apps
effects: Wide spacing, refined serifs, muted gold accents, slow fades
a11y_notes: Thin elegant type and gold-on-light pairs risk failing contrast
perf_notes: Large hero imagery is the main weight so compress and lazy-load

best_for: Consumer apps and kid or family products
effects: Big radii, bright palette, bouncy springs, soft shadows
a11y_notes: Bright cheerful pairs can be low contrast so test text combinations
perf_notes: Spring motion and shadows are modest if kept to transforms

best_for: Engineering tools and technical documentation
effects: Blueprint grid lines, monospace labels, hairline strokes
a11y_notes: Hairlines and small mono text can be hard to read so size up body
perf_notes: Vector grids are light but dense line work can crowd the DOM

best_for: Creative portfolios and approachable explainers
avoid_for: Formal enterprise and precise data UIs
effects: Rough strokes, wobble lines, hand-lettered accents, jitter motion
a11y_notes: Irregular hand-lettering hurts legibility so keep body text in clean fonts
perf_notes: Many hand-drawn SVG paths or images can add notable weight

best_for: Travel real estate and lifestyle landing pages
effects: Full-bleed photos, gradient overlays, parallax, slow zoom
a11y_notes: Text over photos needs scrims and overlays to hold contrast
perf_notes: Large hero images dominate weight so use responsive and modern formats

best_for: Event pages campaigns and bold statements
effects: Oversized display type, dramatic scale jumps, tight layout
a11y_notes: Extreme scale and tight tracking can reduce reading ease at body sizes
perf_notes: Display web fonts add weight so subset and limit families
